title: TC001-Resume upload with target role begins processing
description: Verifies a signed-in user can upload a resume, select a target role, and see processing begin.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Open the Sign in page so I can authenticate as a user.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/header/div/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Open the Profile page to locate the resume upload control and target-role selection.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/header/div/nav/a[2]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Processing')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The page should show a processing indicator after the resume is submitted"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

The Profile page does not provide a resume upload control or a way to select a target role, so the user cannot upload a resume or start processing.

Observations:
- The Profile page displays 'No profile data found.' and no upload or resume-related UI is visible.
- The header shows 'Targeting ML Engineer' as static text, but no interactive target-role selector was found on the page.
- There is no file input, 'Upload resume' button, or any control to submit a resume for processing.

title: TC002-Sign in and reach onboarding resume upload
description: Verifies a job seeker can sign in and access the authenticated onboarding experience to start resume ingestion.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign in' link to open the login page so we can enter credentials.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/header/div/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Open the Profile page to locate the onboarding / resume upload and target role inputs.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/header/div/nav/a[2]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the Dashboard link to look for the onboarding or resume ingestion UI (a 'Start onboarding', 'Start your atlas', or resume upload + target role inputs).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/header/div/nav/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the Dashboard link to look for the onboarding or resume ingestion UI (start onboarding, resume upload, target role inputs).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/header/div/nav/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Upload your resume')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The onboarding should show an Upload your resume prompt so the job seeker can upload a resume and set a target role."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

The onboarding/resume ingestion experience is not available after signing in.

Observations:
- The Profile page shows 'No profile data found.' and contains no controls for starting onboarding or uploading a resume.
- The site navigation (Dashboard, Profile, Roadmap, Gaps, Jobs) does not expose any 'Start onboarding' or 'Upload resume' entry point.
- Authenticated state is present (avatar and Profile active), but there is no visible resume ingestion or target role input UI.

title: TC003-Resume processing completes and dashboard shows structured profile
description: Verifies the user can wait for resume processing to complete and then view the resulting structured profile on the dashboard.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow

        # -> Open the onboarding/resume upload flow by clicking 'Start your atlas'.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/section/div/div/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Skills')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The dashboard should display the structured profile Skills section after resume processing completes."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The onboarding flow requires signing in with Google and there is no alternative email/password sign-in available to continue.

Observations:
- On the onboarding page the only sign-in control is 'Sign in with Google'.
- No email/password or other sign-in option is present, so the onboarding steps (resume upload, role selection) cannot be reached without OAuth.

title: TC004-Generate roadmap from completed analysis and view results
description: Verifies a signed-in user can generate a roadmap from an existing profile and gap analysis and sees the roadmap outputs rendered on the dashboard.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Open the Sign in page by clicking the 'Sign in' link (element index 33).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/header/div/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Open the Gaps page to trigger gap analysis by clicking the 'Gaps' navigation link (element index 286), then wait for the page to update.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/nav/div/a[4]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the Roadmap navigation link to open the Roadmap page and verify that a generated roadmap output (learning steps, projects, checklist items, priorities) is rendered.

        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/header/div/nav/a[3]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

TEST FAILURE

title: TC008-Dashboard route guard redirects unauthenticated users to login
description: Verifies unauthenticated users cannot access the dashboard and are routed to the login experience.

import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():

    browser = None

        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers

title: TC011-Job search prevents duplicate concurrent runs
description: Verifies the UI prevents or safely handles repeated job search attempts while a search is already running.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign in' link to reach the login page so we can authenticate and continue the workflow.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/header/div/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the 'Jobs' navigation link to open the Jobs page and locate the job search controls so we can start a job search and then attempt to trigger a second search while the first is running.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/header/div/nav/a[5]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Enter a search query into the Jobs page search field and submit it (press Enter) to start a job search, so we can then attempt a second search while the first is running.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('ML Engineer')
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Searching jobs...')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The UI should show a single job search in progress indicator after triggering a search and prevent starting a second concurrent search."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC014-Invalid credentials prevent sign-in
description: Verifies a user entering invalid credentials cannot sign in and is shown an authentication error state.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Open the login page by clicking the 'Sign in' link.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/header/div/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Navigate to /login and observe whether the login form is accessible. If redirected because the user is signed in, locate and use the sign-out control first, then proceed to the login page.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Invalid email or password.')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "An authentication error message should be visible after attempting to sign in with invalid credentials"
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/login' in current_url, "The page should have navigated to /login after attempting to sign in with invalid credentials"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
